introSection: Namazu is a steel launched motorbike roller coaster located at Vulcania in Saint-Ours, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Namazu was set to open on 31 May 2021, but the opening was delayed to July.

The ride officially opened on 10 July 2021.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: 2 trains with 6 cars per train. Riders are arranged 2 across in a single row for a total of 12 riders per train.

sections.title: Etymology
sections.content: The name "Namazu" refers to a mythical giant catfish carrying the islands of Japan. The fish is kept in control by the god Kashima. When Kashima's guard slips, Namazu causes earthquakes.

fullText:
Namazu is a steel launched motorbike roller coaster located at Vulcania in Saint-Ours, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France.

Namazu was set to open on 31 May 2021, but the opening was delayed to July.

The ride officially opened on 10 July 2021.



2 trains with 6 cars per train. Riders are arranged 2 across in a single row for a total of 12 riders per train.

The name "Namazu" refers to a mythical giant catfish carrying the islands of Japan. The fish is kept in control by the god Kashima. When Kashima's guard slips, Namazu causes earthquakes.
